atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports left shoulder pain

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports a past left arm surgery

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient reports left shoulder pain exacerbated by specific movements

atomic_propositions.speaker: patient
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient previously used Tylenol for a few days

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation with movement of the left shoulder

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The x-ray shows no fracture or acute bony injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The patient's left shoulder pain is likely due to musculoskeletal injury

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician prescribes ibuprofen 600 mg three times daily for one week

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician advises ice or heat application as needed

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician schedules a follow-up appointment in three weeks

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician advises patient to avoid heavy lifting or strenuous activity for at least three to four weeks

atomic_propositions.speaker: clinician
atomic_propositions.proposition_text: The clinician recommends repeat x-ray if symptoms do not improve after three weeks
